reasoning: Libya has not ratified the AfCFTA as of late April 2025, leaving only a two-month window before the July 1, 2025 deadline. Given Libya's severe, ongoing political fragmentation between rival governments in the east and west, passing and depositing the ratification of an international trade treaty requires a level of legislative and executive coordination that is currently entirely absent. Since there are absolutely no recent reports of legislative momentum or intent to ratify the agreement in the near term, the probability of this occurring within two months is extremely low.
